---
title: "arm_b_5p1m_KD.qmd"
author: "Krish Desai"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## **Reference Download and Preparation**

```{R}
#Address
URL <-"https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/genomes/refseq/fungi/Saccharomyces_cerevisiae/latest_assembly_versions/GCF_000146045.2_R64/GCF_000146045.2_R64_genomic.fna.gz"

#Destenatoin file (output)

dest_file <- "GCF_000146045.2_R64_genomic.test.fna.gz"

#Download the file with wget
download.file(URL, method = "wget", destfile = dest_file)

con <- gzfile("GCF_000146045.2_R64_genomic.test.fna.gz", "rt")
ygen <- readLines(con)
close(con)

# 2. Define the new UCSC-style headers (chrI through chrXVI + chrM)
new_headers <- c(paste0(">chr", as.roman(1:16)), ">chrM")

# 3. Find and Replace
header_lines <- grep(">", ygen)
#Look at the header for chromosome 1
header_lines[1]

# Safety Check: Yeast has 16 nuclear chromosomes + 1 mitochondrial
if(length(header_lines) == 17) {
  ygen[header_lines] <- new_headers
  writeLines(ygen, "S288C_UCSC.fna")
  message("SUCCESS: Reference renamed to UCSC standards.")
} else {
  stop("Error: Chromosome count mismatch! Check your input file.")
}
```

# **Part 2: The Reconstruction (Bash)**

## **Step 1: Assembly with Flye & Log Audit**

```{bash}
flye /Users/kdesai01/Desktop/Genmics_and_transcriptomics/Week2_Yeast_Project/data/clean_reads/ERR1539001_Clean.fastq.gz --outdir data/assamble_genomes --genome-size 12m --threads 8  --scaffold --iterations 5
```
